hook.text: Granite domes peel like onions. Sheets of rock curve away from the surface, leaving smooth, rounded landforms behind.

buildup.text: Exfoliation happens when pressure is released as overlying rock erodes away. The rock expands and cracks in sheets.

discovery.text: Half Dome in Yosemite is a classic exfoliation dome. Curved sheets of granite have peeled away over millions of years.

twist.text: Exfoliation can also be driven by temperature changes. Hot days and cold nights stress rock surfaces until they flake.

climax.text: Exfoliation turns angular rock into rounded domes. It is one of the key processes that sculpts granite landscapes.

punchline.text: Mountains shed their skin like onions.

quiz.question: What causes exfoliation in rock?
options: Pressure release causing rock to expand and crack in sheets
